data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 1 AM ET
data.description:
U.S. Stocks End Lower After Optimism Over Rate Cuts Wanes

The post-Fed rally sputtered Thursday, with major U.S. stock indexes ending lower after an up-and-down session.

Japan Inflation Slows But Uptrend Backs Case for Rate Hikes

Japan's consumer inflation slowed slightly in February due to energy subsidies but maintained the uptrend keeping interest-rate hike expectations intact.

Turkey's Central Bank Raises Overnight Lending Rate in Unscheduled Meeting

The move comes after the arrest of Istanbul mayor Ekrem Imamoglu, the top political rival to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.

Russia Touts Economic Opportunities. Western Companies Are Wary.

Moscow has suggested a restoration of ties holds opportunities for American businesses, but many would hesitate to return.

Bank of Canada Must Stop Tariff-Fueled Price Increases From Spreading, Macklem Says

The remarks are likely to reinforce the market's view that the central bank is headed for a pause in April stemming from a hot inflation reading.

Home Sales Unexpectedly Rose in February

Sales of existing homes rose 4.2%, reflecting an increase in shopping activity and in the number of homes for sale since the start of the year.

U.S. Treasury Sanctions Network Supporting Iranian Oil Exports

The sanctions are part of the administration's "campaign of maximum pressure on Iran," the department said.

South African Reserve Bank Holds Main Repo Rate at 7.5% Amid Rising Uncertainty

The South African Reserve Bank held its main repo rate at 7.5% on Thursday, as tensions with the U.S. continue to rise amid escalating global uncertainty.

U.S. jobless claims still showed few private-sector layoffs, but more ex-federal workers got benefits

The private sector is still culling very few jobs, the latest report on jobless claims shows, but more federal workers fired by the Trump administration are applying for benefits.

France to Put Extra $1.85 Billion Into Defense via Public Investment

State-owned financial institutions will invest the funds in the country's defense companies as European countries prepare for a shakeup of the continent's security order.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 1 AM ET
data.description:
Google's Cybersecurity Deal Spins Tiny Investment Into $4 Billion Windfall

Index Ventures is set to earn a 250-fold return on its seed money in a startup that Google has agreed to buy.

FedEx Cuts Outlook, Despite Higher Profit, Sales

FedEx cut its outlook for the year, citing weak demand for business-to-business shipments stemming from continued weakness and uncertainty in America's industrial economy.

Nike Forecast Steeper Fourth-Quarter Sales Drop in Early Days of Turnaround

Nike expects its sales decline to accelerate as it continues to push discounts to clear inventory in the hopes of stoking a turnaround.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Apologizes for His Quantum Effect

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang offered a quantum-level climb-down on Thursday, after spooking quantum-computing companies earlier this year with his assessment that their efforts wouldn't be "very useful" for 15 to 30 years.

Energy Capital's Partnership With Abu Dhabi's ADQ Also Seen Profiting KKR

A $25 billion investment agreement with the ADQ sovereign-wealth fund will also lift the private-equity firm's $50 billion collaboration with KKR, ECP's founder says.

Boeing Sued for Wrongful Death by Family of Whistleblower

Lawsuit by family of John Barnett blames jet maker for 2024 suicide; Boeing says it is saddened by his death.

Carnival's Earnings Are a Test of Consumer Confidence. Tune In Friday.

The cruise operator's earnings will provide an update on the health of the travel industry but they could be equally important as an indicator about the broader economy.

Ben & Jerry's Founders Say They Stand Behind Ousted CEO

The executive's dismissal is latest dust-up in a yearslong conflict between the brand and its parent company, Unilever.

Tesla Recalls Most Cybertrucks

More than 46,000 of the electric vehicles are being recalled over the potential for an exterior panel to fall off.

Accenture Begins to Feel the Trump Crackdown on Consultants

GSA has asked federal agencies to justify which consulting contracts are mission critical.

data.title: Italy - Factors to watch on March 21
data.description:
The following factors could affect Italian markets on Friday.

Reuters has not verified the newspaper reports, and cannot vouch for their accuracy. New items are marked with (*).

For a complete list of diary events in Italy please click on .

ECONOMY

Bank of Italy releases January data on foreign investors holdings of Italian government bonds.

DEBT

Treasury announces sale of BTP Short and BTPei bonds, with relative amounts to be auctioned on March 26.

COMPANIES

BANCA MONTE DEI PASCHI (MPS)

The Italian lender expects to receive all the relevant approvals to launch its takeover offer for Mediobanca by the end of June, Chief Executive Luigi Lovaglio said on Thursday.

UNICREDIT

Italy's second-largest bank said on Thursday that it had received the non-objection letter from Ireland's central bank to acquire a controlling stake in the insurer BBPM LIFE DAC, a unit of Banco BPM for which Unicredit made a bid.

INTERPUMP GROUP

Releases FY results (Q4 results released on Feb. 14).

DIARY

Prime minister Giorgia Meloni attends European Council meeting in Brussels.

Rome, conference "Water: care of the resource and universal access" (0900 GMT); expected attendees include Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani, Environment and Energy Security Minister Gilberto Pichetto Fratin, U.N. Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) Vice Director General; via videomessages U.N. Special Envoy for Water Retno Marsudi and EU Commissioner for Environment, Water Resilience and circualr and Competitive Economy Jessika Roswall.

data.title: German upper house of parliament expected to clear huge spending package
data.description:
Key points:
  • Upper house expected to pass 500 bln euro fund, debt brake reform
  • Vote is final major hurdle for passing of legislation
  • Package would end decades of fiscal conservativism in Germany

Germany's Bundesrat upper house of parliament is set to vote on Friday on a spending splurge aiming to revive growth in Europe's largest economy and scale up the military for a new era of European collective defence.

The vote in the Bundesrat, which represents Germany's 16 states, is the last major hurdle the package has to clear before being signed into law, unleashing billions of euros into an economy that has contracted for two consecutive years.

Earlier this week, the legislation passed the Bundestag lower house after its initiators, the conservatives and Social Democrats (SPD), secured the support of the Greens for a two-thirds majority. It is widely expected to pass the Bundesrat.

The package would create a 500 billion euro ($546 billion) fund to spend on infrastructure over 12 years and ease constitutionally enshrined borrowing rules - the so-called debt brake - to allow higher spending on defence.

The conservatives and SPD, who are in talks to form a coalition after last month's election, are racing to push it through the outgoing parliament. They fear it could be blocked by an enlarged contingent of far-left and far-right lawmakers in the next Bundestag starting March 25.

Chancellor-in-waiting Friedrich Merz has justified the tight timetable with the rapidly changing geopolitical situation, namely shifts in U.S. policy under President Donald Trump, which European leaders fear could leave the continent exposed to an increasingly hostile Russia and assertive China.

Should the legislation pass the Bundesrat, that would hand Merz a major win before he is even sworn in as chancellor. The 69-year-old, whose conservatives won last month's election, hopes to conclude coalition talks with the SPD by Easter.

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: Markets Digest Central Bank-Filled Week
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

U.K. public sector finances, France monthly business survey, EU flash consumer confidence indicator; no major corporate trading updates expected.

Opening Call:

European stock futures were lower after Asia stocks were mixed. The dollar strengthened. Gold was flat and oil rose.

Equities:

European equity futures pointed to a lower open after Wall Street saw a sputtering rally following the Federal Reserve's decision to pause rate cuts.

Though investors gained insight into the Fed's outlook for interest rates and the economy, major questions remain about President Trump's tariff and immigration policies and their effect on the economy.

"The lack of a clarity that businesses have eventually translates into the clarity that the market has," said Kevin Gordon, senior investment strategist at Charles Schwab. "That makes it difficult for the bull market to resume in a strong way."

Forex:

The U.S. dollar strengthened but faces risk-off sentiment spurred by overnight losses in European and U.S. equity markets.

The greenback benefits from a safe-haven bid amid fragile sentiment against a backdrop of trade policy uncertainty, said NAB's Rodrigo Catril.

The release of the U.S.' America First Trade report on April 2 is seen to be the "big dark cloud," that limits risk appetite, the senior FX strategist said.

Bonds:

Municipal bonds issuance in 2025 is expected to surpass last year's already high level, while demand will be uncertain, BNY's John Flahive said. He expects as much as $600 billion in new issues hitting the market this year, up from more than $500 billion in 2024.

"You see a lot of universities, a lot of heathcare debt coming through," he said.

Investors, meanwhile, worry about tax policy discussions in Congress that could make munis less appealing, he said, adding that "the demand is spotty and not enough to take over the supply."

Energy:

Oil rose amid geopolitical tensions that boost the risk of supply disruptions, after Hamas delivered its first response to three days of Israeli assaults on the Gaza Strip, launching a volley of rockets at Tel Aviv on Thursday.

"The prospect of an extended U.S. campaign against the Houthis combines with Israel's renewed Gaza offensive to put oil squarely back in the spotlight," IG's Chris Beauchamp said.

"Geopolitical tensions continue to be oil's friend," the chief market analyst added.

Metals:

Gold prices were flat as the precious metal took a breather after hitting a record high following Fed Chairman Powell's speech.

Despite flagging a high degree of uncertainty around President Trump's policy changes, Powell's speech didn't change market expectations about the path of monetary policy and the likelihood remains that there will be at least two to three rate cuts this year, said XS.com's Samer Hasn.

-

Copper prices rose, hovering near record-high levels. With the base metal having briefly crossed the psychological level of $10,000 a ton, traders continued to assess the impact of potential tariffs.

Prices are expected to remain supported in the near term due to tightening in the ex-U.S. physical market as Washington's investigation into copper imports continues, said ING strategists.

-

Iron ore fell, with short-term fundamentals and market expectations continuing to show divergent trends, as the outlook for demand remains unclear, said analysts at Nanhua Futures.

Weak sentiment is likely to continue adding pressure to prices, they added.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Russia Touts Economic Opportunities. Western Companies Are Wary.

Russia is dangling the promise of new investment opportunities for American companies as the Trump administration seeks an end to the war in Ukraine, from energy and critical minerals to space cooperation with Elon Musk.

But the opportunities for Western businesses will likely be limited, even if there is a full restoration of economic ties with Moscow. President Vladimir Putin has put caveats on potential cooperation, and many Western firms would hesitate to make new investments as they weigh the political uncertainties and reputational risks.

U.S. Treasury Sanctions Network Supporting Iranian Oil Exports

The U.S. Treasury Department on Thursday stepped up actions to enforce sanctions against Iranian crude oil, including designating a Chinese refining company and its chief executive.

The department said the Shandong Shouguang Luqing Petrochemical Co., a so-called teapot refinery in China's Shandong province, has bought Iranian oil worth about $500 million, including from vessels linked to Yemen's Houthi rebels and the Iranian Ministry of Defense of Armed Forces Logistics.

Heathrow Airport Closes Because of Power Outage

London's Heathrow Airport, a hub for international travel, was shut down for the day on Friday and expects significant disruption in the days to come because of a power outage.

Heathrow will be closed until 11:59 p.m. local time on Friday, according to a statement on the airport's website. Heathrow advised passengers not to travel to the airport and to contact their airlines for more information.

Google's Cybersecurity Deal Spins Tiny Investment Into $4 Billion Windfall

Shardul Shah got the call he had been waiting for on his 37th birthday in January 2020.

A promising Israeli cybersecurity founder that he had been asking for years to launch a company was in need of funding. "It's time," the voice on the other end of the line said. Shah kicked in $3.5 million on behalf of Index Ventures.

Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Apologizes for His Quantum Effect

SAN JOSE, Calif.-Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang offered a quantum-level climb-down on Thursday, after spooking quantum-computing companies earlier this year with his assessment that their efforts wouldn't be "very useful" for 15 to 30 years.

Huang's comments in January triggered a selloff in quantum-computing stocks, with some shares falling 40% or more.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

Expected Major Events for Friday

00:01/UK: Mar UK Consumer Confidence Survey

05:30/NED: Mar Consumer confidence survey

05:30/NED: Feb House Price Index

07:00/DEN: Mar Consumer expectations

07:00/UK: Feb Public sector finances

07:45/FRA: Mar Monthly business survey (goods-producing industries)

08:00/SPN: Jan Industrial Orders & Turnover

08:00/CZE: Mar Business cycle survey (consumer/business confidence)

08:30/POR: Jan Balance of Payments

10:00/BEL: Mar Consumer Confidence Survey

10:00/ITA: Jan Balance of Payments

11:00/IRL: Feb WPI

15:00/EU: Mar FCCI Flash Consumer Confidence Indicator

All times in GMT. Powered by Onclusive and Dow Jones.

Write to us at newsletters@dowjones.com

We offer an enhanced version of this briefing that is optimized for viewing on mobile devices and sent directly to your email inbox. If you would like to sign up, please go to https://newsplus.wsj.com/subscriptions.

This article is a text version of a Wall Street Journal newsletter published earlier today.

data.title: Tunisian president sacks prime minister, presidency says
data.description: Tunisian President Kais Saied sacked Prime Minister Kamel Maddouri and appointed Sarah Zaafarani Zenzari as his replacement, the Tunisian presidency said on Friday.

data.title: Gorilla Gold Extends Gold Mineralization at Western Australia Projects
data.description:
Gorilla Gold said reverse circulation drilling reported new gold intercepts at the Lakeview Prospect within the Comet Vale Project in Western Australia, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Results include 20 meters at 6.1 grams per tonne grade of gold from 153 meters from a wider intercept of 96 m at 2.5g/t Au from 125 m, the filing said.

Drilling at the project continues with one rig, set to expand to two rigs in two weeks, the filing added.

Gorilla Gold also said growth drilling at its Mulwarrie project intercepted new gold lodes from the surface.

Results yielded 7 m at 8.5 g/t grade of gold from 188 meters and 4 m at 2.7 g/t Au from 28m.

Two RC rigs are operating at Mulwarrie, with results expected regularly in the coming months, the company added.

data.title: Xingye Silver & Tin Mining Ties Up With China Railway's Core Member to Develop Mining Technologies
data.description:
Inner Mongolia Xingye Silver & Tin Mining signed a cooperation agreement with China Railway Tunnel Group, a core member of China Railway , according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Friday.

Under the three-year deal, the parties will develop tunnel boring machines for the construction of metal mining, as well as other mining technologies, the Chinese metal manufacturer said.

data.title: Turkey - Factors To Watch on March 21
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Friday.

The lira traded at 38.0000 against the U.S. dollar at 0453 GMT, unchanged from its close on Thursday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 0.50% lower at 9,810.94 points on Thursday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian stocks inched lower on Friday in a subdued end to the week as deepening geopolitical worries and fears over U.S. tariffs and their impact on the global economy curbed investors appetite for risk, keeping safe-haven gold near record highs.

DETENTION OF ISTANBUL MAYOR

Thousands of Turks ramped up protests on Thursday despite a ban on street gatherings over what they called the undemocratic detention of Istanbul's mayor Ekrem Imamoglu, as the opposition sought to pin the blame on President Tayyip Erdogan.

Turkey's main opposition leader told Reuters on Thursday any move to legally bar Istanbul's detained mayor from running for president would only strengthen support for their party, and that any candidate would beat President Tayyip Erdogan in elections.

BANK SHARES

Turkey's banking index (.XBANK) extended losses to slide more than 3% on Thursday amid growing concerns that the central bank may delay or pause future interest rate cuts following Wednesday's sharp lira tumble.

CENTRAL BANK

Turkey's central bank said on Thursday that it suspended one-week repo auctions and hiked its overnight lending rate to 46%, in a move that economists say amounts to a tighter policy stance.

ERDOGAN PROGRAMME

Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan will hold a phone call with Bakir Izetbegociv, leader of Bosnia's Party of Democratic Action (SDA). He will then attend an event in Istanbul for the Kurdish Newroz spring festival. Erdogan will also take part in an online meeting of leaders, and then hold a Ramadan fast-breaking dinner with Guinea-Bissau's President Umaro Sissoco Embalo. (1145 GMT) (1200 GMT) (1445 GMT) (1600 GMT)

MAIN OPPOSITION

Ozgur Ozel, head of the main opposition Republican People's Party (CHP), will visit Istanbul's Beylikduzu municipality, and later address crowds at the Istanbul Municipality headquarters in Sarachane. (0800 GMT0 (1730 GMT)

FOREIGN MINISTER

Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan will host Hungarian counterpart Peter Szijjarto in Ankara.

JUSTICE MINISTER

Justice Minister Yilmaz Tunc will attend a ceremony for the foundation-laying of a judicial building in the central province of Tokat and later attend a series of meetings there. (1030 GMT)

VICE PRESIDENT

Turkish Vice President Cevdet Yilmaz and Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus Prime Minister Unal Ustel will attend a ceremony for the signing of an agreement on financial cooperation.

CONSUMER CONFIDENCE (TRCONC=ECI)

The Turkish Statistics Institute will announce March consumer confidence index data (0700 GMT).

FOREIGN ARRIVALS (TRTARR=ECI)

The tourism ministry will release foreign arrivals data for February (0800 GMT).

WAIVER FOR GAS PAYMENTS TO RUSSIA

Turkey and Slovakia have received extensions from the United States to waivers for gas payments to Russia, sources told Reuters on Thursday, adding that the extensions would be valid until May.

PKK DISARMAMENT

The main political ally of President Tayyip Erdogan said on Thursday that outlawed Kurdistan Worker's Party (PKK) should convene a congress until May 4 to disband itself in line with thecall from the jailed militant leader Abdullah Ocalan.

BLACK SEA NAVIGATIONAL SAFETY

Russian and U.S. experts will discuss ways to ensure the safety of shipping in the Black Sea at talks on a possible Ukrainian peace settlement in Riyadh on Monday, the Kremlin said.

data.title: Australia's TPG Telecom eyes best week in 7 months on Vocus deal approval
data.description:
** TPG Telecom gains as much as 1.8% to A$4.905, highest since October 14

** Stock up more than 8% for the week; on course to post its best week since August 26, 2024

** Australia's competition regulator approves Vocus Group's takeover of TPG's fibre, fixed assets

** TPG gains ~9% YTD

data.title: Gulf Investment Flags Trading Halt on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
Gulf Investment House (ADX:GIH) shares will temporarily pause trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange from 2:30 pm local time on March 26 due to its board of directors meeting.

The financial services company will resume trading after it submits its meeting results to the bourse, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Factors to watch on March 21
data.description:
Britain's FTSE 100 CURRENCYCOM:UK100 index is seen higher on Friday, with futures up 0.07%.

* HEATHROW: Britain's Heathrow Airport said it would be closed all of Friday after a huge fire at a nearby electrical substation wiped out power.

* BOE: BOE Governor Andrew Bailey said that the central bank would have to be careful about cutting interest rates because the fall in inflation pressures has been very gradual.

* CONSUMER CONFIDENCE: British consumer morale ticked higher for a second month as people turned a little more optimistic about the economic outlook, the GfK Consumer Confidence Index showed .

* ENERGY: GB Energy will lead a 200 million pound ($258.72 million) solar panel project for hospitals and schools, Britain said.

* TRADE: Britain is considering linking its Emissions Trading System with the European Union's carbon market.

* BAE: The U.S. State Department has approved BAE Systems as the principal contractor for the potential sale of advanced weapon systems to Saudi Arabia for about $100 million, the Pentagon said.

* VACCINES: A panel that advises the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and is scheduled to review vaccines like GSK's meningococcal vaccine and AstraZeneca's flu shot, will convene in April a spokesman for the Department of Health and Human Services said.

* COMMODITIES: Oil prices and were set for second consecutive weekly gains, while gold was headed for a third straight week of gains and copper prices in London were pressured by a stronger dollar.

* The UK blue-chip index CURRENCYCOM:UK100 closed flat on Thursday.

data.title: Taiwan's Export Orders Jump 31.1% in February
data.description:
Taiwan's export orders rose 31.1% annually to $49.5 billion in February, the Central Bank of Taiwan (Republic of China) reported Thursday.

In local currency, export orders were NT$1.622 trillion, up 36.8% versus a year earlier.

data.title: Luxshare Precision Industry to Acquire More Wingtech Subsidiaries
data.description:
Luxshare Precision Industry further bought subsidiaries under Wingtech Technology , according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Thursday.

A lump sum of 1.20 billion yuan will be paid on the signing date and the net asset value of the target companies, including the final debt-to-equity swap amount, will be paid on the closing date.

The Chinese electronic components manufacturer will buy Huangshi Wingtech Communications, Kunming Wenyao Electronic Technology, Wingtech Technology (Shenzhen), Wingtech Technology Indonesia, Wingtech Group (Hong Kong), and Kunming Wenxun Industrial.

The agreement also includes business asset packages that were managed by the Wingtech subsidiaries, some of which were involved in the consumer electronics system integration business.

Luxshare's shares fell more than 1% in recent trade.

data.title: India's Sensex Up 0.3%, Led by Financial, Industrial Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: Indian shares are higher as fears about U.S. tariffs ebb for now. Financial and industrial stocks are leading the gains. Bajaj Finance is up 2.4% and Kotak Mahindra Bank is 1.0% higher. Larsen & Toubro is up 0.8% and NTPC is 1.0% higher. Among the individual movers, Ola Electric Mobility is up 6.4% after saying its sales remain strong and that a temporary backlog in February is being cleared. Investors are focusing on any developments in U.S. trade and foreign policies and their implications for Indian businesses. The benchmark Sensex is up 0.3% at 76566.85. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Oil set for second straight weekly gain on Iran sanctions, planned OPEC+ cuts
data.description:
Oil prices rose on Friday, and were set for second consecutive weekly gains, as fresh U.S. sanctions on Iran and a new plan from the Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries and its allies (OPEC+) to cut output raised bets on tighter supply.

Brent crude futures climbed 21 cents, or 0.3%, to $72.21 per barrel by 0435 GMT. U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures were up 25 cents, or 0.4%, to $68.32 a barrel.

On a weekly basis, both Brent and WTI were on track to rise about 2%, their biggest weekly gains since the first week of 2025.

The United States Treasury on Thursday announced new Iran-related sanctions, which for the first time targeted an independent Chinese refiner among other entities and vessels involved in supplying Iranian crude oil to China.

The sanctions on Chinese entities were "a clear escalation in sanctions policy", analysts at RBC Capital Markets said in a note on Friday.

"While the physical implications are minimal, we think it reasonable that risk premium here is taken more seriously," they wrote.

That marked Washington's fourth round of sanctions against Iran since U.S. President Donald Trump in February promised to reimpose a "maximum pressure" campaign on Tehran, pledging to drive the country's oil exports to zero.

Analysts at ANZ Bank said they expect a 1 million barrels per day (bpd) reduction in Iranian crude oil exports because of tighter sanctions. Vessel tracking service Kpler pegged Iranian crude oil exports at over 1.8 million bpd in February.

Oil prices were also supported by a new OPEC+ plan announced Thursday for seven members to further cut output to make up for producing more than agreed levels. The plan would represent monthly cuts of between 189,000 bpd and 435,000 bpd, and will last until June 2026.

OPEC+ earlier this month confirmed that eight of its members would proceed with a monthly increase of 138,000 bpd from April, reversing some of the 5.85 million bpd of output cuts agreed in a series of steps since 2022 to support the market.

"While the group shares a plan for compensation cuts, it certainly doesn't mean members will follow it. A handful of members have consistently produced above their target production levels," ING analysts said in a note on Friday.

data.title: Heathrow Airport Closes Because of Power Outage — WSJ
data.description:
By Hannah Miao

London's Heathrow Airport, a hub for international travel, was shut down for the day on Friday and expects significant disruption in the days to come because of a power outage.

Heathrow will be closed until 11:59 p.m. local time on Friday, according to a statement on the airport's website. Heathrow advised passengers not to travel to the airport and to contact their airlines for more information.

The outage was caused by a fire at an electrical substation supplying the airport, according to Heathrow.

Heathrow doesn't know when power will be reliably restored and will provide an update when more information is available, a spokesperson said.

"We expect significant disruption over the coming days and passengers should not travel to the airport under any circumstances until the airport reopens," the spokesperson said.

The fire broke out late Thursday evening at an electrical substation in western London, according to the London Fire Brigade. Around 70 firefighters were battling the fire, which also caused a power outage affecting many homes and local businesses, the brigade said.

The cause of the fire is currently unknown, according to the brigade.

Friday's closure affects at least 1,300 flights scheduled to and from Heathrow, according to flight tracker Flightradar24.

As of early Friday morning local time, 120 aircraft already in the air would need to divert to alternate airports or return to their origins, according to Flightradar24.

Heathrow was the fourth busiest airport in the world last year ranked by capacity with more than 51.5 million seats, according to data provider OAG. Last year, 83.9 million passengers traveled through Heathrow, according to the airport.

Write to Hannah Miao at hannah.miao@wsj.com

data.title: Chinese Non-Financial Outbound Direct Investments Rise 9% in January-February
data.description:
Chinese non-financial outbound direct investment rose 9.1% to $22.97 billion in January and February, Xinhua News Agency reported Thursday, citing the Commerce Ministry.

Overseas project revenues by Chinese companies slipped 5.6% to $18.3 billion, while the value of new contracts jumped 29% to $35.3 billion, the report said.

In countries covered by the Belt and Road Initiative, non-financial ODI grew 18% to $5.52 billion during the two months combined, the report said.

Contracted project revenues by Chinese firms in Belt and Road countries slipped 5.2% to $15.1 billion, while new contract values increased 34% to $30.9 billion, Xinhua said.

data.title: VSTECS Holdings: Will Continue Executing Strategies to Increase Market Share, Range of Products, Services >0856.HK
Indian bond yields steady as traders await fresh cues
data.description:
Indian government bond yields were little changed in early deals on Friday, as investors awaited fresh triggers after this week's decline.

The benchmark 10-year bond yield (IN067934G=CC) was at 6.6256% as of 10:10 a.m. IST, compared with its previous close of 6.6366%.

"The benchmark yield may gradually shift down towards 6.60% till March end," a trader at a primary dealership said.

"Benign monetary policy outlook, lack of supply with the central bank purchasing debt, likely rate cut and stance change may drive the 10-year yield to 6.55% in April."

The yield on the 10-year note dropped to the lowest level since January 21, 2022, at Thursday's close after the strengthening rupee boosted bets of a rate cut by the Reserve Bank of India next month.

The RBI policy decision is due on April 9. It had cut its key interest rate for the first time in nearly five years in February.

The Indian rupee has advanced more than 1% so far in March, making it a leader among major Asian currency peers.

Investors also cheered the U.S. Federal Reserve's policy decision this week, after it left interest rates unchanged but maintained that it is likely to deliver two quarter-point rate cuts later this year.

Market sentiment was also supported by strong demand during state debt sale and another surprise debt purchase plan announced by the RBI.

The Indian central bank is set to buy bonds worth 500 billion rupees ($5.80 billion) next week, to add to its 1 trillion rupee purchase in March.

Foreign investors have also stepped up purchases of Indian government bonds, with their total investment in bonds that are a part of global indexes crossing 100 billion rupees in March.

These banks net bought bonds worth 119 billion rupees under Fully Accessible Route till March 20, according to clearing house data.

($1 = 86.2675 Indian rupees)

data.title: Nan Yang Dyeing & Finishing Decides Against Paying Dividend
data.description:
Nan Yang Dyeing & Finishing will not distribute dividends, following a board resolution, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The company, engaged in dyeing and finishing of fiber products, did not disclose further details regarding the decision.

data.title: Fubon Financial Unit Decides Against Dividend Distribution
data.description:
Fubon Financial said Fubon Bank (China) will not distribute dividends, following a board resolution, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The financial services provider did not disclose further details regarding the decision.
